title: GOOD AGRICULTURAL PRACTICES (GAPs) FOR SMALL MILLETS
abstract:
Finger millet is generally grown as rainfed crop in Semi-arid tropics and sub-humid globally due to its natural ability to survive under less rainfall (500 to 1000 mm, annually) and low inputs. The following GAPs are prepared accordingly. However, finger millet can perform better under well-endowed environments and can respond to high inputs.
Land preparation: Achieve fine tilth by plowing 2-3 times (primary and secondary cultivation) and level the field to prepare ridges (30 – 60 cm apart) and furrows or raised (15cm) broad beds of 1.5 m width.
Weeds are a major problem in small millets as the grassy weeds resemble the crop. After primary cultivation, irrigate the field to allow the weeds to germinate. Wait for 15 days
and cultivate the field to prepare for sowing. This way, weeds can be prevented for up to two weeks.
